description:
You are a compliance analyst who must update a controlled Git repository and then generate a concise audit-trail file so that the security team can automatically ingest it.

    Repository that already exists:
      • Path: /home/user/projects/policy-docs
      • Remote: none (local-only; you do NOT need to push anywhere)
      • Current content:
          /home/user/projects/policy-docs/policy.md
          └─ Contains several paragraphs (leave everything intact, just append a new line).

    Your objectives (perform them only from inside the repository directory):

      1. Append exactly the following new line (with the leading “* ” and final period) to the bottom of policy.md:
         * All stored data must be encrypted at rest.

      2. Stage and commit the change with the exact commit message:
         Add encryption standard note

      3. Produce a one-commit audit-trail file named:
         /home/user/audits/latest_audit.log
         (Create the /home/user/audits directory if it does not exist.)

         The file must contain ONE and only one block describing the most recent commit
         (“HEAD”) in the following, line-oriented, machine-readable format (four lines, no
         blank lines, no extra whitespace, no ANSI color codes):

             CommitHash: <40-char SHA1>
             Author: <author name> <email in angle brackets>
             Date: <YYYY-MM-DD HH:MM:SS ±TZ>
             Message: Add encryption standard note

         • “CommitHash:” must be followed by the full 40-character SHA-1 of the new commit.
         • “Author:” must be the default Git author line exactly as reported by
           `git log --format='%an <%ae>' -1`.
         • “Date:” must reflect the commit date as shown by
           `git log --date=iso --format='%ad' -1`.
         • “Message:” is the fixed string shown above.

      4. Confirm that /home/user/audits/latest_audit.log exists and
         its contents strictly follow the four-line specification. The automated
         grader will parse this file verbatim, so any deviation (extra spaces,
         missing colon, additional blank lines, etc.) will fail.

    NOTE:
      • Only standard Git commands are necessary (≈2-3 commands total).
      • You do NOT need to configure a remote or interact with any network resources.
      • Do NOT change any files other than policy.md and the specified log file.
      • You do NOT need superuser privileges.
truth:
Initial repository state before the agent begins:

      Directory: /home/user/projects/policy-docs  (already a Git repo)
      Branch: master (checked out, clean)
      HEAD commit hash: e2d2d78a8ecf18f3d3cdd671b7f1b1d8f8020000
      File tree of that commit:
        policy.md  (contains at least one line, ends with newline)

    Permissions:
      /home/user/projects  – user-writable
      /home/user/audits    – does NOT exist yet but user has write permission to /home/user

    Verification steps used by the automated test:

      1. Confirm that /home/user/projects/policy-docs is still a Git repository.
      2. Run `git log --no-decorate -1 --format='%H'` and store the new HEAD hash H.
         Expect H != e2d2d78a8ecf18f3d3cdd671b7f1b1d8f8020000.
      3. Check commit message of H equals “Add encryption standard note”.
      4. Verify that the final line of /home/user/projects/policy-docs/policy.md
         is exactly:
            * All stored data must be encrypted at rest.
      5. Read /home/user/audits/latest_audit.log and assert it matches the exact
         regex (PCRE notation, anchors included):

         ^CommitHash:\s+([0-9a-f]{40})\nAuthor:\s+(.+ <.+>)\nDate:\s+[0-9]{4}-[0-9]{2}-[0-9]{2} [0-9]{2}:[0-9]{2}:[0-9]{2} [+-][0-9]{4}\nMessage:\s+Add encryption standard note\n$

         Additionally, capture group 1 (hash) must equal H.

    If all assertions pass, the task is marked as successful.
name: task_000000_31275728
